channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Marcos personally distributes rice to Manila families under relief program
item.description: President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr. led a rice distribution program in Manila on Thursday, providing immediate food assistance to families amid rising economic pressures caused by tensions in th...

item.title: Review: Rajkummar Rao shines in Hindi comedy ‘Toaster’
item.description: The Hindi film 'Toaster', directed by Vivek Daschaudhary and streaming on Netflix, is a comedy centred around Ramakant (Rajkummar Rao), a man known for his extreme stinginess. He is obsessed with gett...

item.title: Self-enumeration by households begins for Census 2027 in 11 states and UTs
item.description: In preparation for Census 2027, approximately 12 lakh households across 11 states and Union Territories have already completed self-enumeration for the house-listing operations phase. This self-enumer...

item.title: Abhishek Sharma and Axar Patel join NADA's testing pool for April-June 2026
item.description: India’s T20 World Cup-winning cricketers Abhishek Sharma and Axar Patel have been added to the National Anti-Doping Agency’s (NADA) Registered Testing Pool (RTP) for the second quarter of 2026. They r...

item.title: Nairobi must dismantle parking cartels siphoning city revenue
item.description: The article argues that Nairobi’s parking system has been taken over by cartels that collect fees from motorists yet hardly any of that money goes to City Hall. These gangs operate in the CBD and othe...

item.title: Sherrod Brown builds substantial fundraising lead over Jon Husted in Ohio Senate race
item.description: The article details how former Democratic Senator Sherrod Brown has amassed a significant financial edge over Republican incumbent Senator Jon Husted in the 2026 Ohio U.S. Senate contest. New campaign...

item.title: Air New Zealand introduces lie-flat bunk beds for long-haul flights
item.description: Air New Zealand is set to transform long-haul travel with the launch of its new Skynest sleeping pods on the Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner. Positioned between the Economy and Premium Economy cabins, the Sky...

item.title: Justice Jackson Publicly Criticizes Supreme Court's Handling of Emergency Orders
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson has delivered a sharp critique of her conservative colleagues’ increasing use of emergency orders to facilitate policies enacted by the Trump administration...

item.title: Unseasonal Western Disturbances Damage Rabi Crops Across India
item.description: Western disturbances, cyclonic winds typically active in winter, have struck India unusually in March and April this year, bringing heavy rain and hailstorms that have damaged crops across northern an...

item.title: Woman in Yaletown stabbing cases given conditional sentence and probation
item.description: Tamara Jean Hamelin, the woman who pleaded guilty to multiple random assaults and stabbings in Vancouver’s Yaletown neighbourhood in October 2025, has been sentenced to a conditional sentence with no ...

item.title: Vancouver Mayor Proposes Exploring Potential Major League Baseball Franchise
item.description: Vancouver Mayor Ken Sim has announced his intention to explore the possibility of bringing a Major League Baseball (MLB) team to the city. In a recent statement, Sim emphasized Vancouver's strong spor...

item.title: Downtown Edmonton group proposes strategies to improve city safety
item.description: A downtown advocacy group in Edmonton is calling on city council to adopt measures aimed at making Edmonton the safest major city in Canada. The Downtown Revitalization Coalition's report identifies o...

item.title: Minister urges trustees to uphold accountability and proper asset management
item.description: In Songea, the Tanzanian Minister for Constitution and Legal Affairs, Dr Juma Zuberi Homera, reminded boards of trustees about their legal duty to manage institutional assets responsibly. Speaking at ...

item.title: Supreme Court Remarks on Sabarimala Reform Discussions
item.description: Okay, so the Supreme Court, they were hearing some stuff about the Sabarimala Temple case, right? And what they said, it's quite something. Basically, they told everyone that you can’t just go and hol...

item.title: Four Suspects Arrested in Athi River During Anti-Drug Operation
item.description: In Athi River, four people have been arrested following a well-coordinated anti-narcotics operation carried out by Kenya's Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI). The suspects, identified as Mar...

item.title: PDP Governorship Aspirant Outlines Plans to Tackle Lagos State Challenges and Boost Inclusiveness
item.description: Adelaja Adeoye, one of the main governorship aspirants for the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), don come out clear say if Lagos people give am chance, e go tackle the plenty socio-economic wahala wey d...

item.title: Babachir Lawal Alleges APC Wants One-Party Rule, ADC Vows to Contest 2027 Elections
item.description: For real, Babachir Lawal, wey be former Secretary to the Government of the Federation and top person for African Democratic Congress (ADC), don yan say the APC-led Federal Government dey push Nigeria ...

item.title: Johannesburg Court Orders Musa Khawula to Retract False Claims About Julius Malema
item.description: In Johannesburg, the Gauteng High Court has ruled that media commentator Musa Khawula must retract false statements he posted on social media regarding Julius Malema and his wife. The judge deemed Kha...

item.title: Canadian Softwood Producers Pay Over US$8 Billion in U.S. Duties Since 2017
item.description: Since 2017, Canadian softwood producers have paid over US$8 billion in duties to the United States, with accumulated interest bringing the total to more than US$10 billion. The issue stems from longst...

item.title: Understanding Dysfunctional Breathing and Its Impact on Health
item.description: Breathing is often seen as an automatic process, yet many adults experience dysfunctional breathing, which can occur independently of any disease. Dysfunctional breathing, or breathing pattern disorde...
